name: Onogoro Island
description: Onogoro Island (淤能碁呂島, Onogoro-shima) is a sacred island in Japanese mythology, significant as the first landmass created by the divine couple Izanagi and Izanami. Formed from the chaotic primordial waters, it served as the foundation for the creation of the Japanese archipelago and the birthplace of many deities. Izanagi and Izanami descended from the heavens and stirred the waters with the Amenonuhoko (heavenly jeweled spear). When they lifted the spear, drops fell from its tip, solidifying into Onogoro Island. This island became their home and the site of their marriage, marking the beginning of the creation of the Japanese islands and the divine lineage. The exact location of Onogoro Island is debated, with some suggesting it represents a symbolic or spiritual place rather than a specific geographic location. Its importance lies in its role as the origin point, the first step in the creation of the world according to Japanese mythology, and a testament to the power of creation and divine union. The island is intrinsically linked to the story of Izanagi and Izanami's creation myth and the subsequent birth of the Japanese islands and deities. It represents the genesis of the world and the beginning of Japanese civilization.
subtitle: The First Island, Birthplace of Creation

keyMyths.title: Creation of Onogoro
keyMyths.description: Izanagi and Izanami stirred the primordial waters with the Amenonuhoko, and the drops falling from the spear solidified into Onogoro Island. This marked the first land and the beginning of their creation of Japan.

keyMyths.title: Izanagi and Izanami's Marriage
keyMyths.description: After the creation of Onogoro Island, Izanagi and Izanami built a palace and performed a marriage ritual. This union led to the birth of the Japanese islands and numerous deities.
